<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.size_constraint_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.xss_match_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--xss_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.rate_based_statement.scope_down_statement.regex_pattern_set_reference_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--regex_pattern_set_reference_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rate_based_statement--scope_down_statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_match_statement"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.regex_match_statement`

Optional:

- `field_to_match` (Attributes) Field of the request to match. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match))
- `regex_string` (String)
- `text_transformations` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_match_statement--text_transformations))

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.regex_match_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--regex_match_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--rule_group_reference_statement--rule_action_overrides--action_to_use"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.rule_group_reference_statement.rule_action_overrides.action_to_use`

Optional:

- `allow` (Attributes) Allow traffic towards application. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rule_group_reference_statement--rule_action_overrides--action_to_use--allow))
- `block` (Attributes) Block traffic towards application. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rule_group_reference_statement--rule_action_overrides--action_to_use--block))
- `captcha` (Attributes) Checks valid token exists with request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rule_group_reference_statement--rule_action_overrides--action_to_use--captcha))
- `challenge` (Attributes) Checks that the request has a valid token with an unexpired challenge timestamp and, if not, returns a browser challenge to the client. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rule_group_reference_statement--rule_action_overrides--action_to_use--challenge))
- `count` (Attributes) Allow traffic towards application. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rule_group_reference_statement--rule_action_overrides--action_to_use--count))
- `monetize` (Attributes) Monetize action for rules. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--rule_group_reference_statement--rule_action_overrides--action_to_use--monetize))

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--size_constraint_statement"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.size_constraint_statement`

Optional:

- `comparison_operator` (String)
- `field_to_match` (Attributes) Field of the request to match. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match))
- `size` (Number)
- `text_transformations` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--size_constraint_statement--text_transformations))

<a id="nestedatt--rules--statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules.statement.size_constraint_statement.field_to_match`

Optional:

- `all_query_arguments` (String) All query arguments of a web request.
- `body` (Attributes) The body of a web request. This immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--body))
- `cookies` (Attributes) Includes cookies of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--cookies))
- `header_order` (Attributes) The string containing the list of a web request's header names, ordered as they appear in the web request, separated by colons. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--header_order))
- `headers` (Attributes) Includes headers of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--headers))
- `ja3_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA3 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--ja3_fingerprint))
- `ja4_fingerprint` (Attributes) Includes the JA4 fingerprint of a web request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--ja4_fingerprint))
- `json_body` (Attributes) Inspect the request body as JSON. The request body immediately follows the request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--json_body))
- `method` (String) The HTTP method of a web request. The method indicates the type of operation that the request is asking the origin to perform.
- `query_string` (String) The query string of a web request. This is the part of a URL that appears after a ? character, if any.
- `single_header` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--single_header))
- `single_query_argument` (Attributes) One query argument in a web request, identified by name, for example UserName or SalesRegion. The name can be up to 30 characters long and isn't case sensitive. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--single_query_argument))
- `uri_fragment` (Attributes) The path component of the URI Fragment. This is the part of a web request that identifies a fragment uri, for example, /abcd#introduction (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules--statement--size_constraint_statement--field_to_match--uri_fragment))
- `uri_path` (String) The path component of the URI of a web request. This is the part of a web request that identifies a resource, for example, /images/daily-ad.jpg.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_wafv2_web_acl.example
  identity = {
    name  = "name"
    id    = "id"
    scope = "scope"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
### Identity Schema

#### Required

- `id` (String) Id of the WebACL
- `name` (String) Name of the WebACL
- `scope` (String) Use CLOUDFRONT for CloudFront WebACL, use REGIONAL for Application Load Balancer and API Gateway

#### Optional

- `account_id` (String) AWS Account where this resource is managed
- `region` (String) Region where this resource is managed

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_wafv2_web_acl.example
  id = "name|id|scope"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
$ terraform import awscc_wafv2_web_acl.example "name|id|scope"
```
